label: First Hour (Prima)
description: The Byzantine First Hour (Prima) is the morning office concluding the all-night vigil cycle, sung at dawn after Orthros. On ordinary days without a vigil it follows immediately after Orthros. It sanctifies the first hour of the day with fixed psalmody, a troparion of the day, the Theotokion of the hour, and the Prayer of the First Hour.

sections.label: Opening
sections.notes: The First Hour opens with the Usual Beginning, as do all Byzantine offices.

items.text: The Priest blesses: Blessed is our God, always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

items.label: The Usual Beginning
items.note: Trisagion prayers sequence. Resolved from first-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: Fixed Psalmody
sections.notes: The First Hour appoints three fixed psalms: 5, 89, and 100 (LXX). These are invariable on ordinary days.

items.label: Psalm 5 (LXX) — Give ear to my words, O Lord
Psalm 89 (LXX) — Lord, Thou hast been our refuge
Psalm 100 (LXX) — Of mercy and judgment I will sing

sections.label: Trisagion Prayers
sections.notes: The Trisagion prayer sequence precedes the troparia.

sections.label: Troparia
sections.notes: The dismissal troparion of the day is sung. The tone-dependent Theotokion of the First Hour follows. On ferial days the troparion is from the Octoechos or Menaion; on Sundays the Resurrectional Troparion of the current tone.

items.label: Troparion of the Day
items.note: Variable troparion: resolved via Menaion resolver or weekday-theme rubric by HorologionEngine. Same infrastructure as Vespers troparion slot.

items.label: Theotokion of the First Hour
items.note: Variable: tone-dependent Theotokion from the Octoechos First Hour cycle. Resolved by HorologionEngine as explicit rubric stub at baseline; full text deferred pending First Hour Octoechos data.

sections.label: Prayer of the First Hour and Dismissal
sections.notes: The Prayer of the First Hour concludes the fixed psalmody cycle. The office ends with the standard dismissal.

items.label: Prayer of the First Hour
items.note: Fixed invariable prayer. Resolved from first-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

items.text:
Priest: Christ our true God, through the intercessions of His all-pure Mother, of all the Saints, have mercy on us and save us, as He is good and the Lover of mankind.
Choir: Amen.
